hops.context: As we will shortly present in Section V, the specific Detector helping E-commerce users will utilize a community-owned similarity map, denoted as Auxiliary Database in our framework, to choose the items to purchase when probing the platform.
Since the Auxiliary Database is also owned by the Web3 community, every update of this database must also be recorded in the blockchain.
Since the Auxiliary Database is also owned by the Web3 community, every update of this database must also be recorded in the blockchain.

question: In the context of an E-commerce platform, how does the Detector's use of the Auxiliary Database influence its functionality?

options.A: The Detector leverages the Auxiliary Database to store user data securely, complying with data protection regulations.
options.B: The Detector uses the community-owned Auxiliary Database to select items for users, ensuring choices align with community updates.
options.C: The Detector utilizes the Auxiliary Database to enhance its processing speed by accessing pre-stored similarity maps.
options.D: The Detector interacts with the Auxiliary Database to independently create marketing strategies without community input.

explanation: The Detector utilizes the Auxiliary Database, which is owned by the Web3 community and contains a similarity map, to select items for users. This ensures that the choices made are always updated and aligned with the latest community preferences, as every update to the database is recorded on the blockchain.
context: As we will shortly present in Section V, the specific Detector helping E-commerce users will utilize a community-owned similarity map, denoted as Auxiliary Database in our framework, to choose the items to purchase when probing the platform. | Since the Auxiliary Database is also owned by the Web3 community, every update of this database must also be recorded in the blockchain. | Since the Auxiliary Database is also owned by the Web3 community, every update of this database must also be recorded in the blockchain.

hops.context: In future research, it is imperative to broaden the application of the proposed evidence generation framework.
We conduct a case study on recommendation systems with systematic evaluation experiments using two real-world datasets.
We conduct a case study on recommendation systems with systematic evaluation experiments using two real-world datasets (i.e., the Amazon Magazine dataset and the Amazon Beauty dataset).

question: Which dataset type did the future research aim to extend the evidence generation framework using, particularly evaluated in the context of recommendation systems?

options.A: A dataset primarily composed of electronic product reviews and customer feedback.
options.B: A synthesized dataset based on generic e-commerce transaction patterns.
options.C: A controlled experimental dataset focusing solely on beauty product reviews.
options.D: A real-world dataset derived from magazine sales and preferences on Amazon.

explanation: The correct answer is A because the knowledge path indicates that future research aims to extend the evidence generation framework, which was evaluated using the Amazon Magazine dataset, identified as a type of dataset. This type of dataset is real-world, derived from magazine sales and preferences on Amazon, making it specific to the context mentioned in the paper.
context: In future research, it is imperative to broaden the application of the proposed evidence generation framework. | We conduct a case study on recommendation systems with systematic evaluation experiments using two real-world datasets. | We conduct a case study on recommendation systems with systematic evaluation experiments using two real-world datasets (i.e., the Amazon Magazine dataset and the Amazon Beauty dataset).
